plan.what: Prepare and publish a new PyPI release for stepgate by bumping the package version from 0.2.0 to 0.2.1, cleaning the package metadata text that still contains corrupted punctuation, rebuilding the distribution artifacts, and uploading the new release to PyPI.
plan.why: PyPI already has stepgate 0.2.0 published on July 14, 2026, so the current changes cannot be uploaded under the same version. The package metadata in pyproject.toml also still contains corrupted punctuation, so fixing that before the next release avoids republishing broken text on the project page.
plan.how: Update the version string and clean the affected metadata text in the packaging files, build fresh sdist and wheel artifacts, validate them with twine, and then upload the new 0.2.1 release to PyPI using the credentials already configured on this machine without printing secrets to the terminal.
plan.expected_result: PyPI will have a new stepgate 0.2.1 release whose package metadata reflects the cleaned English text and whose artifacts are built from the current repository state.
plan.verification: Confirm the local build succeeds, twine validation passes, the upload completes successfully, and PyPI reports stepgate 0.2.1 as the latest release.

data.summary: bumped stepgate to 0.2.1, cleaned the package summary metadata, built fresh distribution artifacts, validated them with twine, and uploaded the new release to PyPI
data.git_diff_stat:
.stepgate/history.jsonl                    |  2 ++
 .stepgate/sessions/codex-2026-07-13-1.json | 53 ++++++++----------------------
 pyproject.toml                             |  2 +-
 src/stepgate/__init__.py                   |  2 +-
 4 files changed, 17 insertions(+), 42 deletions(-)

data.evidence: python -m build produced dist/stepgate-0.2.1.tar.gz and dist/stepgate-0.2.1-py3-none-any.whl, python -m twine check dist/* passed, twine upload completed successfully and returned https://pypi.org/project/stepgate/0.2.1/, and an HTTP HEAD request to that 0.2.1 release URL now returns 200 OK.

pending_suggestion: refresh the published PyPI long description once the package page cache catches up, to confirm the README shown there reflects the latest docs changes
